AMENDED POS OF MEMO OF P & A IN SUPPORT OF OPPOS. TO MTN FOR GOOD FAITH STTLEMENT; DEC AND (PROPOSED)ORDER FILED BY DEFENDANT PACIFIC UNION INTERNATIONAL, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT VLADIMIR RUBASHEVSKY'S AMENDED MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT IS GRANTED. SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE SUPPORTS A FINDING OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT BASED ON THE FACTORS SET FORTH IN TECH-BILT, INC. V. WOODWARD-CLYDE & ASSOCIATIATES (1985) 38 CAL. 3D. 488. PLAINTIFF SEEKS APPROXIMATELY $40,000 IN DAMAGES. SETTLORS' PAYMENT OF $10,000 IS IN THE BALL PARK. THERE IS INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE OF COLLUSION. THE REQUEST FOR DISCOVERY IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants To Further Respond To Requests To Produce Documents, Request For Special Interrogatories And Request For Admissions, And For An Order For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-16-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Oly Filatova, Esq (415-568-8677) for Ilona Brusil Plaintiff and moving party
No other appearance on the matter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling with modifications. PLAINTIFF ILONA BRUSIL's Motion To Compel Defendants To Further Respond To Requests To Produce Documents, Request For Special Interrogatories And Request For Admissions, And For An Order For Sanctions is partially Granted and Denied. The tentative ruling is adopted with addition of response required to special interrogatories. Sanctions of $850 is awarded. Prevailing party to submit order approved as to form. Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Mtn For Order Determining Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Apr-23-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Rebecca M. Hoberg, Esq. of Weyand Law Firm, Tel. 415.536.2800, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT PACIFIC UNION INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S Motion For Order Determining Good Faith Settlement is granted. No opposition filed. Order signed. Judge: A. James Robertson; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

Amended Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Jul-29-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MIKALAI HROSHAU, ESQ., 415.684.8660, FOR PLAINTIFF ILONA BRUSIL.
RICHARD CARRINGTON, ESQ., 415.861.1434, FOR DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANTS NIKOLAY BUSEL & IRINA BUSEL.
J. SCOTT FEDER, ESQ., 415.536.2800, FOR DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT IGOR RUCHLER.
TANYA GOMERMAN, ESQ., 415.545.8608, FOR CROSS DEFENDANT VLADIMIR RUBASHEVSKY.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS DEFENDANT VLADIMIR RUBASHEVSKY's Amended Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement is granted. Substantial evidence supports a finding of good faith settlement based on the factors set forth in Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associatiates (1985) 38 Cal. 3d. 488. Plaintiff seeks approximately $40,000 in damages. Settlors' payment of $10,000 is in the ball park. There is insufficient evidence of collusion. The request for discovery is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
